zh.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville於1100年出生在英國艾可。他於1130年與Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville于1100年出生在英国艾可。他于1130年与Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Maundeville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Gabrielle, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Gaston, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Michel.

French: habitational name from Mandeville, the name of two places in Normandy, derived from Latin magna villa ‘large estate’, or a variant of Manneville, a habitational name from Manneville, the name of several places in Normandy (see 2 below). Compare Montville and Monville .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Manneville (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), Manneville-sur-Risle (Eure), or Magneville (Manche).

variants.name: Mansfield
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire. The placename means ‘open land by the river Maun’, from the river name Maun (which may take its name from a hill named with Old English mamme ‘teat’) + Old English feld ‘open country’.
Irish (Waterford): when not an importation of 1, this is an altered form of the Norman name de Manville (see Mandeville 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Mansfeld, a habitational name from a place so called in Saxony.

variants.name: Manderville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manwell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manville
meanings: English (Sussex and Lancashire): variant of   Mandeville  .
Altered form of   Mainville   1, a surname of Swiss French origin (see   Miville  ).
French: variant of Menville, a habitational name from Menville in Haute-Garonne, and also of Mainville 2.

variants.name: Mundell
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .
Americanized form of German Mündel (or some other similar, like-sounding surname): nickname for someone with a prominent mouth, from Middle High German munt, mündelīn ‘(little) mouth’.

variants.name: Monville
meanings: Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
Walloon: habitational name from Monville in Namur province in Wallonia (Belgium), of the same etymology as in 3 below.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Montville in Seine-Maritime or Monville in Calvados, both named with Old French mont ‘mountain, hill’ + ville ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Mandal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Chandi, Sanjay, Sankar, Somnath, Aditi, Aditya, Ajit, Amala, Amitabha, Anil, Anirudh, Aparna.
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit maṇḍala, a word that has several meanings, including ‘sphere’ and ‘halo’.
English (Cumbria; of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Montville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Vernice.
Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
French: variant of   Monville  . This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Creuse.

variants.name: Manfull
meanings: English (Midlands): probably a variant of   Mandeville  . Compare   Manville  .

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville was born in 1100, in Essex, England. He married Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville in 1130, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He was buried in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville est né en 1100, à Essex, Angleterre. Il a épousé Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville en 1130, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Ilenterré à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville wurde 1100 in Essex, England geboren. Er hat 1130 in England Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er wurde in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville è nato il nel 1100 nel Essex, England. Lui sposato Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville nel 1130, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Fu sepolto a Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.New Temple Churc, Holborn, Suffolk, ENGL: イギリスイギリススフォルク
ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundevilleは1100年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた。1130年にイギリスでMrs Geoffrey de Maundevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリススフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville(은)는 1100년 영국 에세스에서 태어났다. 그는 1130년 영국에서 Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville nasceu em 1100, em Essex, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville em 1130, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele foi sepultado em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville родился в 1100 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville в 1130 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он был похоронен в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Maundeville nació en 1100, en Essex, Inglaterra. Se casó con Mrs Geoffrey de Maundeville en 1130, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Fue sepultado en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
